# Enterprise Specification: Stochastic Modeling and Statistical Methods Standard

---

## 1. Purpose

This document defines the enterprise standard for stochastic modeling, statistical analysis, and probabilistic methods.

The objective is to ensure:

* Statistically sound decision-making
* Reproducible analytical results
* Proper uncertainty handling
* Consistent modeling practices

---

## 2. Scope

This specification applies to:

* Statistical modeling
* Probabilistic simulations
* Risk modeling
* Data science and analytics workflows

---

## 3. Core Principles

* Use of well-defined probability models
* Explicit handling of uncertainty
* Reproducibility of statistical analyses
* Transparency in assumptions

---

## 4. Probability Models

* Models MUST clearly define distributions
* Assumptions MUST be documented
* Random variables MUST be explicitly defined

Common distributions:

* Normal distribution
* Binomial distribution
* Poisson distribution
* Exponential distribution

---

## 5. Statistical Methods

* Hypothesis testing MUST follow defined significance levels
* Confidence intervals SHOULD be reported
* Sampling methods MUST be documented
* Bias and variance MUST be considered

---

## 6. Simulation

* Monte Carlo methods MAY be used for complex systems
* Random seeds MUST be controlled for reproducibility
* Simulation parameters MUST be documented

---

## 7. Model Validation

* Models MUST be validated using independent data
* Sensitivity analysis SHOULD be performed
* Results MUST be interpretable and explainable

---

## 8. Tooling

Approved tools MAY include:

* Python (NumPy, SciPy, pandas)
* R
* Statistical computing frameworks

---

## 9. Governance

* Statistical models MUST be reviewed
* Assumptions MUST be documented
* Analytical results MUST be auditable

---

## 10. Further Reading

* [Khan Academy Statistics](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/statistics-probability?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
* [SciPy Documentation](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
* [Introduction to Probability (MIT)](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-05-introduction-to-probability-and-statistics-spring-2014?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

---

## 11. Summary

This standard ensures rigorous, transparent, and reproducible statistical and stochastic modeling practices.
